capability_statement: Operators can inspect provider execution cost and quota boundaries before provider-backed execution advances.
before_state: AOF could show a controlled provider candidate and incident recovery drill, but spend and quota limits were not a first-class release gate.
after_state: AOF records and audits budget owner, budget period, cost ceiling, token/call/retry caps, quota, rate limit, billing, overage policy, and stop governance.
scenario: Before a provider-backed GitHub/Jira/Slack/OpenAI action advances, an operator runs provider-cost-quota-boundary-audit and sees whether spend and quota are bounded or execution remains blocked.
five_minute_demo: Run provider-cost-quota-boundary-audit and release-state-audit from a clean checkout.
time_saved_or_work_reduced: The operator no longer reconstructs spend and quota risk from approval, production-boundary, and provider notes manually.
cognitive_load_removed: The operator can distinguish cost/quota boundary evidence from production execution permission.

capability_matrix.before: Budget and quota were scattered boundary text.
capability_matrix.after: Budget owner, cost ceiling, token/call/retry caps, quota/rate-limit, billing, overage, and stop governance are audited.
capability_matrix.user_value: Users can see whether provider execution is allowed to spend tokens, calls, quota, or money before execution advances.

capability_matrix.before: Provider candidate had incident recovery but no spend gate.
capability_matrix.after: Provider candidate must have incident recovery and cost/quota evidence before advancing.
capability_matrix.user_value: Users can avoid treating provider execution readiness as budget approval.

not_proven: This value evidence does not prove market adoption, actual provider billing correctness, actual quota enforcement, or production execution safety.
